items.title: Initiate planning for Cambridge 400 and convene Cambridge 400 Advisory Committee
items.summary: The Council asked the City Manager to initiate planning for Cambridge 400 (the city's 400th anniversary) and to convene a Cambridge 400 Advisory Committee with members representing the range of stakeholders needed to guide the work. Adopted with amendments by voice vote; Councillor Zusy was added as a sponsor.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to initiate the planning process for Cambridge 400 and as part of this, to convene a Cambridge 400 Advisory Committee composed of members representing the range of stakeholders whose participation is needed to guide this work. COUNCILLOR SIMMONS, MAYOR SIDDIQUI. POR 2026-66

items.title: Proclaim April 6–12, 2026 as Cambridge Public Health Week
items.summary: The Council went on record proclaiming April 6–12, 2026 as Cambridge Public Health Week in appreciation of City government, community organizations, health professionals, businesses, and residents working on health equity. Adopted unanimously.
items.body: That the City Council go on record proclaiming April 6–12, 2026 as Cambridge Public Health Week in special appreciation of the dedicated work of City government, community organizations, health professionals, businesses, and residents in advancing health equity and building a healthier, more resilient Cambridge. MAYOR SIDDIQUI, COUNCILLOR NOLAN. POR 2026-70

rationale.geo.scope:citywide: Cambridge-wide proclamation
rationale.civic.councillor_vote:azeem:yea: YEAS list includes Vice Mayor Azeem

items.title: Proclaim April 13–20, 2026 as International Dark Sky Week in Cambridge
items.summary: The Council went on record proclaiming April 13–20, 2026 as International Dark Sky Week and called on residents, businesses, institutions, and City departments to raise awareness of light pollution and adopt responsible outdoor lighting practices. Adopted unanimously.
items.body: That the City Council go on record proclaiming April 13–20, 2026 as International Dark Sky Week in the City of Cambridge, and calls upon all residents, businesses, institutions, and City departments to join in observing this important week by raising awareness of light pollution, adopting responsible outdoor lighting practices, and supporting the protection of our shared night sky as a precious and finite resource. MAYOR SIDDIQUI, COUNCILLOR MCGOVERN, COUNCILLOR NOLAN. POR 2026-71

rationale.geo.scope:citywide: proclamation 'in the City of Cambridge'
rationale.topic.area:climate: light-pollution / night-sky protection is environmental policy

items.title: Raise residential parking permit fee to $75; $25 subsidized fee; remove senior exemption; cap at 2 cars
items.summary: A previously tabled policy order proposing major changes to the residential parking permit program: a $75 base fee, a subsidized $25 fee for residents in affordable housing or SNAP, removal of the senior exemption, and a cap of 2 permits per household (down from 4). Taken from the table and adopted as amended by substitution 7-2, with Councillors Flaherty and Simmons voting nay.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments to raise the fee of the parking permit program for all residents to $75, consider how to include a self-identified check off option so as not to increase administrative costs for a subsidized fee of $25 for residents who live in affordable housing, are enrolled in a program such as SNAP or are low income, remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program and lower the number of cars that individual residents are allowed to get a residential parking permit for from four to two. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 9, 2026. PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026. POR 2026-32
amendments: Adopted as Amended by Substitution — final language differs from the agenda body

history.action: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons

rationale.doc.status:adopted_amended: RESULT: Adopted as Amended by Substitution [7-2-0]
rationale.doc.stage:carryover: Carryover from Feb 9 (Charter Right) and March 2 (Tabled)
rationale.geo.scope:citywide: residential parking permit program is citywide
rationale.housing.tenure:any: parking permit fees affect both renters and owners
rationale.housing.affordability:inclusionary: subsidized fee for 'residents who live in affordable housing'
rationale.transport.mode:drive: residential parking permits affect car owners
rationale.demo.age:senior_65plus: remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program
rationale.demo.income:low: subsidized fee of $25 for residents who ... are low income
rationale.demo.income:means_tested: enrolled in a program such as SNAP
rationale.civic.councillor_vote:azeem:yea: YEAS list includes Vice Mayor Azeem

items.title: Awaiting report: expand free early child care offerings, including means-tested models
items.summary: An outstanding report request asking the City Manager to work with relevant departments on processes to expand free early child care offerings in Cambridge, including means-tested programming and non-City funding sources. Still pending.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which Cambridge can expand free early child care offerings, including models for means-tested programming, and exploration of non-City funding sources, from the state or foundations. Councillor Nolan, Councillor McGovern, Mayor Siddiqui. AR 2026-23

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section X. APPENDED INFORMATION / AWAITING REPORT LIST

rationale.demo.income:low: free child care primarily benefits low-income families

items.title: Awaiting report: explore steps toward advancing social housing in Cambridge
items.summary: An outstanding report request asking the City Manager to confer with CDD, Finance, Law, and other departments to explore all steps toward advancing social housing in Cambridge. Still pending.
items.body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to confer with the Community Development Department, Finance Department, Law Department, and other relevant departments to explore all steps towards advancing social housing in Cambridge. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-58. Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler. AR 2026-24

items.title: Awaiting report: discontinue all official City posting and engagement on X within 60 days
items.summary: An outstanding report request asking the City Manager to instruct all City departments, boards, commissions, and offices to discontinue all official posting and engagement on X (formerly Twitter) within 60 days, with an explanation. Still pending.
items.body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to instruct all City departments, boards, commissions, and offices to discontinue all official posting and engagement on X within 60 days; and to include an explanation of why the City will no longer use X. Councillor Al-Zubi, Councillor Nolan. AR 2026-25

items.title: Awaiting report: harmonize Harvard / MASCO institutional bus protocols with public commitments
items.summary: An outstanding report request asking the City Manager to direct CDD and the Transportation Department to meet with Harvard's Office of Community Relations and the Longwood Collective (MASCO) to align institutional shuttle-bus protocols with public-facing commitments. Still pending.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to direct the Community Development Department and the Transportation Department to meet with Harvard's Office of Community Relations and the Longwood Collective (MASCO) to harmonize institutional bus protocols with public-facing commitments. Councillor Al-Zubi, Mayor Siddiqui, Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Councillor Nolan. AR 2026-26

items.title: Awaiting report: study requiring single-stall public bathrooms to be gender-neutral
items.summary: An outstanding report request asking the City Manager to direct relevant City departments and staff to examine and report back on whether the city can require single-stall public bathrooms to be gender-neutral. Still pending.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to direct relevant City departments and staff to examine and report back on whether the city can require single-stall public bathrooms to be gender-neutral. Councillor McGovern, Mayor Siddiqui, Councillor Simmons, Councillor Al-Zubi, Councillor Flaherty, Councillor Nolan, Vice Mayor Azeem. AR 2026-27
